text.title: Database Design (PostgreSQL) Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every Database Design (PostgreSQL) application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Start in 3NF; denormalize ONLY after measuring a specific query bottleneck.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Date — Intro to DB Systems. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Every index MUST trace to a real query in the workload sample (`EXPLAIN ANALYZE`), never speculative.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: PostgreSQL performance docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Integrity rules (FK, CHECK, NOT NULL, UNIQUE) MUST live in the DB, not only in app code.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: PostgreSQL admin best-practice. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Every migration MUST ship a backward `down` step; no one-way migrations in production.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Migration-tool conventions. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Use TIMESTAMPTZ for all wall-clock timestamps; TIMESTAMP (no tz) only for explicit local-time semantics.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: PostgreSQL docs + time-zone postmortems. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: PK type (UUID v7 vs bigint) is a global decision; never mix per table.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Schema-consistency reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Every FK MUST set ON DELETE explicitly (CASCADE / RESTRICT / SET NULL); never rely on default NO ACTION silently.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: PostgreSQL docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
